item.title: Switzerland Asks If 10 Million People Is Where to Draw the Line
item.description: A Swiss plebiscite this weekend on whether to impose a population ceiling is offering voters a choice that threatens to become their most consequential yet of the current century.

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Drones While Talks Continue for Interim Deal
item.description: The US shot down Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz as talks continued for an interim peace deal to reopen the strategic waterway, adding to uncertainty over when an agreement can be reached.

item.title: Korean Media Giant JoongAng’s JTBC Defaults, Downgraded to Junk
item.description: JTBC Co. Ltd., the broadcaster owned by South Korea’s media giant JoongAng Group, defaulted on securitized loans and was downgraded to junk by a local ratings firm, triggering a series of rating cuts across the conglomerate.

item.title: Geely to Shut Some Units, Focus on HK-Listed Arm, Chairman Says
item.description: China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group will streamline its structure by shutting down or merging some entities and concentrate resources around its Hong Kong-listed arm, in a bid to improve governance, Chairman Li Shufu said.

item.title: China Rebukes US Over ‘Military’ Labeling of Its Top Firms
item.description: China’s Commerce Ministry said it firmly opposes the US decision to label additional firms as “military companies,” accusing Washington of using national security as a pretext to curb the development of Chinese firms.

item.title: Why Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPO
item.description: It was meant to happen once humans were regularly flying to Mars. Then the AI boom took off.

item.title: US Kills Tren de Aragua Leader Guerrero in Strike on Compound
item.description: The US killed the leader of the Tren De Aragua drug cartel, Niño Guerrero, in a strike that was carried out with the assistance of Venezuela, President Donald Trump said.

item.title: OpenAI Probed by Coalition of State Attorneys General
item.description: OpenAI is under investigation by a coalition of state attorneys general who requested information from the artificial intelligence company on a wide range of topics.

item.title: CFTC Considers Blocking CME’s 24/7 Oil Contract Bid
item.description: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is considering whether to block CME Group Inc.’s bid to launch a round-the-clock oil contract, heightening tensions between the market stalwart and its regulator.

item.title: What to Know About SpaceX’s Record-Breaking IPO
item.description: SpaceX had the largest stock-market debut in history when it went public on June 12. The company raised $75 billion in the initial public offering and ended its first day on the public markets with a market capitalization of around $2.2 trillion. The IPO turned SpaceX founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire.

item.title: Knicks Win Triggered Record Loss for Susquehanna Sports Traders
item.description: Jeff Yass, the founder of the Wall Street trading firm Susquehanna International Group, has long been a New York Knicks fan. But he couldn’t fully savor the moment when the team completed its epic comeback to win game four of the NBA finals.

item.title: Paramount Purchase of Warner Bros. Cleared by Justice Department
item.description: The US Justice Department has closed its antitrust probe into the $110 billion merger of Paramount Skydance Corp. and Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., according to people familiar with the decision.

item.title: Matt Holt Said to Near $12 Billion Deal for Ensemble Health
item.description: Private-equity executive Matt Holt’s investment firm Thoreau Group is in advanced talks to acquire Ensemble Health Partners in a deal valuing the company at about $12 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: SpaceX Mania Powers New Retail-Risk Complex Beyond Wall Street
item.description: Wall Street just spent the week ricocheting between the macro and the mania.

item.title: SpaceX Passes Tesla as Most Valuable Company in Musk’s Portfolio
item.description: When it comes to the interconnected companies of Elon Musk, Tesla Inc. is no longer the big man on campus.

item.title: ‘Mutual Fund Dark Ages’ Beckon as Brokerage Platform ETF Fees Rise
item.description: Schwab considers new platform fees for ETF issuers

item.title: Biggest Shipment of US Fuel to Cuba Since 1960 Is Off as Blacklist Expands
item.description: A Florida oil trading house that was set to dispatch the largest US fuel shipment to Cuba since 1960 is shelving plans after the Trump administration ramped up pressure on the island.

item.title: Traders Are Most Positive on US Dollar Since February 2025
item.description: Traders turned the most optimistic on the US dollar in more than a year as the war in the Middle East supports the currency’s status as a haven.

item.title: Tesla Holder Merz Sits Out SpaceX IPO in Bet on Musk Merger Bid
item.description: Alexandra Merz, a longtime Tesla Inc. investor, said she deliberately avoided buying shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering because she expects Elon Musk to move quickly to combine the two companies.

item.title: Roku Said to Be in Sale Talks, Including Possible Media Tie-Up
item.description: Roku Inc., the streaming video platform, is in talks to sell itself, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: SpaceX Rented Out Computing After Own Teams Had Trouble Using It
item.description: SpaceX decided to rent out the full capacity of its Colossus 1 data center in Memphis to Anthropic PBC after encountering technical challenges using the facility to develop and run its Grok artificial intelligence models, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: French Wheat Yields Look Better Than Last Year, Grain Group Says
item.description: France’s winter soft wheat and barley yields appear slightly better than last season despite adverse weather, according to Benoit Pietrement, president of industry group Intercereales.

item.title: Why ‘Final Fantasy VII Revelation’ Took Only Three Years to Make
item.description: Director Naoki Hamaguchi says the keys were retaining staff and sticking to his vision

item.title: World Cup’s $10 Billion Betting Boom Fuels Market ‘Gamification’
item.description: The World Cup is expected to be the biggest sporting event on the planet. It may also become the industry’s biggest chance yet to turn a global sporting spectacle into an engine for speculative trading.

item.title: Putin Vows to Expand Starlink Rival With Aim to Step Up Attacks
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said his country is expanding a constellation of domestically produced satellites as he vowed to step up strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure.

item.title: The Four Biggest Issues Facing the Music Industry Right Now
item.description: From AI to fraud to AI-powered fraud, the music business has some concerns

item.title: Dangote Lifts Nigerian Crude Intake as Overseas Buyers Pull Back
item.description: Nigeria’s massive Dangote refinery is boosting purchases of the country’s crude, helping to stem waning demand for grades from West Africa in light of uncertainty over the resumption of oil shipments from the Middle East.

item.title: SpaceX Options to Begin Trading on Tuesday After IPO
item.description: Options contracts on Space Exploration Technologies Corp. will begin trading Tuesday after shares began trading on Friday.

item.title: Rival Space Stocks Tumble as Investors Race Toward Musk’s IPO
item.description: SpaceX’s long-awaited initial public offering triggered a selloff across rocket, satellite and space-linked companies Friday.

item.title: JBS to Close Pennsylvania Beef Plant Amid Cattle Shortage
item.description: JBS NV is closing a beef plant in Pennsylvania, in the latest sign of pressures meatpackers are facing amid a severe US cattle shortage.
